metadata_json.notes: Model-judged score awards near-full credit for correctness, completeness, and adherence to constraints with minor recommendations for clearer spreadsheet-formula visibility and an explicit statement mapping MWh reserve to MW capacity for regulation revenue computations.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Programmatically validate spreadsheet formulas (open the XLSX and show explicit formula cells) so reviewers can verify calculations automatically. Clarify the interpretation that the 20 MWh reserve is treated as 20 MW capacity for regulation revenue (state explicitly that 'reserved 20 MWh' is treated as 20 MW capacity per interval). Include a short section in the memo mapping each spreadsheet cell/range to the corresponding memo line for faster verification.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Required artifacts were produced and the dispatch memo contains a reproducible per-interval dispatch, SOC trajectory, intermediate calculations, revenue breakdown, cycle accounting, and sensitivity scenarios. Numerical checks in the memo are internally consistent: one-way efficiency = sqrt(0.88)=0.9381 applied symmetrically; SOC updates match charge/discharge decisions and one-way efficiency; energy revenues and regulation revenues sum to reported totals; equivalent cycle calculation is consistent with SOC throughput (37.52 MWh / 80 MWh = 0.469  0.47). Constraints are respected (<=20 MW power, SOC maintained at/above 20 MWh reserve, charge only when DA price <50, discharge only when DA price >85).

rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Minor: cycle-accounting calculation method not fully visible in text preview and spreadsheet preview could not be parsed; this reduces ability to fully verify the EFC derivation though the reported value is within limits. No material constraint violations were found.
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Ensure the spreadsheet includes an explicit, labeled calculation for equivalent full cycles (displaying total throughput numerator and division by 80 MWh). Include a small 'validation' tab showing step-by-step SOC, energy-in (MWh charged from grid), energy-out (MWh discharged to market), and the EFC formula. Also make sure the spreadsheet is saved in a format that can be programmatically previewed or include a plain-text CSV export of key calculation tables for automated checks.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present. The memo documents inputs, assumptions, per-interval dispatch, SOC trajectory, revenue breakdown, sensitivity scenarios, and a decision table. Numerical checks visible in the provided previews and raw normalized fields show: (1) charge only in intervals with DA price < $50 (intervals 1 & 2), (2) discharge only when DA price > $85 (intervals 4 & 5), (3) power limits respected (max 20 MW charge/discharge), (4) SOC respects 20 MWh reserve and 80 MWh cap, (5) round-trip efficiency applied symmetrically using sqrt(0.88) per leg (per-leg efficiency 0.938083 matches the charge/discharge SOC calculations), and (6) revenue accounting matches dispatch (charge cost $1,600 = 20*38 + 20*42; gross discharge revenue = 20*105 + 15.2*92 = $3,498.4; regulation revenue = 20 MW * sum(reg prices) = $1,300). Sensitivity analysis is present and consistent with the dispatch rule. The only semantic opacity is the exact formula shown for equivalent full-cycle (efc_used = 0.6266667) in the visible memo/preview; the numeric result is plausible and below the 1.2 limit, but the spreadsheet contents could not be programmatically inspected (openpyxl unavailable) so the explicit throughput formula and line-level formulas were not verified directly.
